lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 2 -- Your Story, Your Credentials

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: the science of how an embryo forms and develops
a fertility doctor who treats hormones and reproduction
being the first to develop something new
medical help to achieve a pregnancy
your qualifications and achievements that prove your expertise
a new business project, often with some risk

reading.rtitle: The 60-Second Story: How Experts Introduce Themselves at the Top
paras: At an executive program or a medical congress, the first question is almost always the same: 'So, what do you do?' The best professionals do not list every job they have ever had. They tell a short, clear story. A strong introduction has five parts: a hook, your role, your area of expertise, what you are working on right now, and a question back to the other person.

Notice the grammar behind a good introduction. When you talk about experience and achievements, you use the present perfect: 'I have worked in fertility care for years,' or 'I have presented at international conferences.' The exact date does not matter; the experience does. But when you mention a finished event with a finished time, you switch to the past simple: 'I did my fellowship in 2016,' or 'I trained at a center in London.'

Finally, confident experts sound human, not robotic. They use natural bridges like 'I am in the process of opening my own clinic,' or 'What I am most excited about right now is our new partnership.' Then they turn the conversation around: 'And what about you?' A great introduction is not a speech. It is the start of a conversation.
reading.source: Adapted from a professional-communication guide, The Executive Voice

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the article?
choices: A good introduction should list every job and qualification you have ever had.
A strong self-introduction tells a short story, uses the right tenses, and starts a conversation.
Experts should memorize one long speech and deliver it the same way every time.

items: A strong introduction has five parts, including a question back to the other person.
The text lists a hook, your role, your expertise, your current focus, and a question back.
You use the past simple to talk about general experience with no specific date.
General experience uses the present perfect; the past simple is for finished events with a finished time.
'I did my fellowship in 2016' is past simple because it is a finished event with a finished time.
The text gives this exact sentence as a past-simple example.
A great introduction should sound like a long, formal speech.
The text says a great introduction is not a speech; it is the start of a conversation.

gapfill.text: Notice: 'I have worked' and 'I have presented' are present perfect for experience with no exact date. 'I did my fellowship in 2016' is past simple because it names a finished time.

bank.label: Natural bridges for your introduction
items: I am in the process of...
What I am most excited about right now is...
My work sits at the intersection of...

lf.title: Experience or finished event?
items: at the American School as a child.

lf.text: Notice: 'have worked' and 'have presented' are present perfect -- general experience, no exact date. 'did' and 'studied' are past simple -- finished events with a finished time (2016, as a child). Match the tense to the kind of information.

items.situation: You are at a welcome dinner. In one or two sentences, say who you are and what you specialize in.
tips: I am a ... and I specialize in ...

items.situation: Someone asks how long you have been in your field. Answer with the present perfect.
tips: I have been in this field for ...

items.situation: Talk about your training. Say where you did your fellowship and when.
tips: I did my fellowship at ... in 2016.

items.situation: Mention one thing you are working on right now, then turn the question around.
tips: Right now, I am in the process of ...

items: Give the five-part introduction: a hook, your role, your expertise, your current focus, and a question back. Use your notes.
Say the same introduction again in under forty-five seconds. Keep only the strongest details.
Now do it with no notes. Sound natural, not memorized, and remember to breathe.
A colleague asks: 'What did you do before fertility medicine?' Answer with the past simple, then bridge back to the present with the present perfect.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model introduction
list: Model bio: 'Hi, I am Eliane. I am a gynecologist and a fertility specialist. I have worked in reproductive medicine for over eight years, and I have presented at several international congresses. I did my fellowship in 2016. Right now, I am in the process of opening my own clinic with cutting-edge technology. And what about you?'
Present perfect (experience, no date): I have worked / I have presented / I have been involved in.
Past simple (finished event + time): I did my fellowship in 2016 / I studied at ... / I trained at ....
Bridges: I am in the process of... / What I am most excited about right now is... / My work sits at the intersection of medicine and business.
answerkey.note: Use the present perfect for experience and achievements; switch to the past simple only when you name a finished time.

listenings.text: Good afternoon. My name is Dr. Camila Duarte, and I am a reproductive endocrinologist. I have worked in assisted reproduction for more than a decade, and I have helped hundreds of families start their journey to parenthood. I did my fellowship at a leading center in Barcelona, and since then I have presented my research at conferences across Europe and the United States. Right now, I am leading a new program in embryology at my hospital. What I am most excited about is how cutting-edge technology is changing what we can offer our patients. Thank you, and I look forward to meeting many of you this week.

Hi Eliane, this is Mark from the executive program office. I am calling to confirm your place at the welcome dinner on Thursday. During the evening, each participant gives a short, one-minute introduction to the group. There is nothing to worry about. Just tell us who you are, what you specialize in, and one project you are excited about right now. Many people have told me it is the best part of the week. If you have any questions, feel free to call me back. We are all looking forward to meeting you. Talk soon.
